paper_title: Synthesis of Au\/UiO-66-NH2\/Graphene composites as efficient visible-light photocatalysts to convert CO2

catalyst.source: The experiments were done under visible light irradiation using 500 W xenon lamp with cutoff filter (l 400 nm) by dispersing 0.02 g photocatalyst in a mixed solution (40 mL DMF, 5 mL triethanolamine-TEOA and 3 mL H 2 O).
catalyst.context: 2-amino-terephthalic acid and zirconium (IV) chloride were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich, Reagent-grade N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF) and methanol were obtained from the Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd and used without treatment.Chloroauric Acid was also purchased from the Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.Graphene was got from the Nanjing XFNANO Materials Tech Co.,Ltd. The composite photocatalyst UiO-66-NH 2 GR was fabricated according reported solvothermal method 43.In a typical synthesis, before the experiment, 50 mL N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF) was prepared firstly in a jar bottle, then 2.0 graphene (2.0 mg graphene) was added in the bottle.The solution was stirred and ultrasonicated for 30 min respectively, then the 90 mg 2-amino-terephthalic acid and 120 mg zirconium (IV) chloride were dissolved in the DMF, along with stirring for 15 min and ultrasonication for another 15 min.The formed mixed solution is then transferred to a 100 mL Teflonlined stainless-steel autoclave.The autoclave was heated in a hot oven at 120 C for 24 h.After cooling down to room temperature, the sample was washed and centrifugated through methanol, DMF and ethanol for several times and put it in the vacuum oven for 12 h at 60 C. The as-obtained composite photocatalyst was named as UiO-66-NH 2 GR. Preparation of photocatalysts AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR and Au UiO-66-NH 2 First, the HAuCl 4 aqueous solution was prepared.The 1 g of HAuCl 4 solid was dissolved in 500 mL deionized water with stirring (concentration of the solution is 2 gL).The solution was then transferred to 500 mL brown volume bottle, wrapped in tinfoil paper on the outer wall and carefully placed in 4 C refrigerator (prevented oxidation discoloration). A quantity of the composite photocatalyst mentioned above was weighed and dispersed in a complex solution containing 40 mL water and 10 mL of methanol (reducing agent).Afterwards, different content of the as prepared HAuCl 4 solution (Theoretical load volume is 2e10, The specific quantity is 2 14 1.255 mL; 4 14 2.51 mL; 6 14 3.765 mL; 8 14 5.02 mL; 10 14 6.275 mL; the weight of UiO-66-NH 2 GR is 60 mg) was added in the complex solution.The mixture was degassed under nitrogen for 15 min in the ice water bath.After that, the air intakes and vents are closed, then it was put under visible light with stirring for different time.The illumination time range is 15 mine90 min respectively.The obtained sample was then centrifuged and washed with water and ethanol for 3 times respectively.The final product was dried under vacuum drying oven at 80 C for 24 h.The as-obtained composite photocatalyst was named as AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR. As a reference, the AuUiO-66-NH 2 was obtained through the first procedure of fabricating the UiO-66-NH 2 , then the 60 mg UiO-66-NH 2 was adopted to deposite the Au nanoparticles, the experimental parameters were kept identical with previous. X-Ray Diffraction (XRD) patterns of the samples were collected on X'pert MPDPro (Panalytical Co.) diffractometer using Cu Ka radiation (40 kV, 40 mA) to analyze the crystal phase of the samples, 2 theta varied from 5 to 80 .Surface electronic states of the samples were performed by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS, Thermo Scientific EscaLab 250Xi, Al-Ka), the binding energy values were calibrated using C 1s 14 284.6 eV as a reference.Morphologies of the products were studied through the field emission scanning electron microscopy (SEM), instrument: HITACHI SU6600 (Japan).More detailed information on the morphologies and energy dispersive spectrometer (EDS) spectra were acquired from a JEOL JEM2100 (Japan) high resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM).The Brunauer Emmett Teller specific surface areas (BET) were studied on a Micromeritics ASAP 2020 device (USA) operating at liquid nitrogen temperature of A196 C. UVevisible (UVevis) diffused reflectance spectroscopies (DRS) were tested on a HITACHI UV4100 spectrometer (800 nm200 nm), reference: BaSO 4 .The apparent quantum yield was measured on Edinburgh FLS9 (China), Excitation range: 373 nme391 nm; Luminescence range: 392 nme801 nm. The electrochemical measurements were conducted on an electrochemical workstation (CHI 660D) in a three-electrode system.The photocatalyst was mixed with polyethylene glycol (PEG, MW 20000) and ethanol, then coated on the conductive surface of the FTO (active area of ca.4.0 cm 2 ) and served as the working electrode, before used, the coated FTO conductive glasses were dried at room temperature and calcined in a tube furnace (200 C, 2 h, N 2 atmosphere).The Platinum sheet was used as a counter electrode, a 500 W Xe lamp (l 400 nm) was employed as the light source, 0.50 molL Na 2 SO 4 aqueous solution was used as the electrolyte. The experiments were done under visible light irradiation using 500 W xenon lamp with cutoff filter (l 400 nm) by dispersing 0.02 g photocatalyst in a mixed solution (40 mL DMF, 5 mL triethanolamine-TEOA and 3 mL H 2 O).The homemade glass reactor was filled with mixture solution and bubbled with the mixing gas (Ar: CO 2 14 1:1) for 30 min.Then the reactor was exposed to the visible light illumination for 4 h at ambient temperature.The CO 2 reduction amount was calculated based on the following equation: CO 2 reduction amount 14 CeC 0 , where C 0 and C refer to the CO 2 amount determined before and after the reaction." The CO 2 was analyzed through gas chromatograph (Agilent Technologies: 6890N) with TCD detector (5A molecular sieve columns).Helium (99.999) was used as the carrier gas.The amount of H 2 was also determined on this gas chromatograph using N 2 as the carrier gas. The liquid product of formic acid was detected by ion chromatography.Ion chromatography (IC-1100) is equipped with CD detector, RFC-30 on-line electrolytic leaching fluid generator, Dionex ionpac AS18 4 A 250 mm chromatographic column.

catalyst.source: AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR nanocomposites were successfully prepared first via the hydrothermal method to introduce the GR and then followed a photoreduction process to deposit the Au-NPs.
catalyst.context: In summary, AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR nanocomposites were successfully prepared first via the hydrothermal method to introduce the GR and then followed a photoreduction process to deposit the Au-NPs.A strong visible light absorption and Raman spectra shift of composite were presented owing to the characteristic SPR effect of Au nanoparticles.The composites exhibited excellent photocatalytic performance over CO 2 reduction under visible-light.Particularly, the 6 AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR exhibited the highest CO 2 activity and selectivity among different samples.The improvement of photocatalytic performance was ascribed to the UiO-66-NH 2 has high specific surface area and large pore volume; the Au-NPs have plasma excitation, which could extend visible light-absorption range and enhance the harvesting intensity of visible light; GR sheets could accept and transfer electrons superbly, the charge separation could be promoted attributing to the fast transportation of charge carriers on the GR, besides, the GR could also enhance the dispersibility of UiO-66-NH 2 and thus lead to the superior capture of CO 2 ; A strong junction and synergetic effect among different materials were strengthened and thus lead to superior photocatalytic performance.This work provides a promising method to fabricate highly efficient photocatalyst, which could be also used in the field of clean energy production, solar water splitting and other photocatalytic systems. Fig. 1 e XRD patterns (a) and Raman spectra (b) of the as-synthesized samples. Fig. 2 e SEM, TEM, HRTEM and mappings images of composites of UIO-66-NH 2 GR synthesized through hydrothermal method and AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR at different magnification; SEM images of UiO-66-NH 2 GR synthesized through hydrothermal method (a), AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR (b); TEM images of UiO-66-NH 2 GR synthesized through hydrothermal method (c), AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR (d), HRTEM of interface in the composites (e). Fig. 3 e XPS survey spectra of different samples (a), high resolution spectra of Zr 3d (b), C 1s (c), N 1s (d), O 1s (e) and Au 4f (f). Fig. 4 e N 2 adsorptiondesorption curves (a) and pore size distributions (b) of the as-prepared samples. Fig. 6 e The evolution and selectivity of formic acid under visible-light (l 400 nm) irradiation (a); The reduction of CO 2 and hydrogen evolution of different samples (b); The reduction of CO 2 over sample 6 AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR after seven cycles (c). Fig. 7 e PL spectra (a); Time-resolved PL spectra (b); Photocurrent spectra (c) and Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (d) of UiO-66-NH 2 , UiO-66-NH 2 GR and 6 AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR. Fig. 8 e Mott-Schottky plots according to impedance measurements (aeb); The schematic diagram for electrons transfer mechanism for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction over photocatalysts (c). Element composition (). The activity of the samples prepared with various of loading content of Au. Photoreduction of CO 2 over graphene-based photocatalysts. i n t e r n a t i o n a l j o u r n a l o f h y d r o g e n e n e r g y 4 6 ( 2 0 2 1 ) 1 1 6 2 1 e1 1 6 3 5 This work was supported by National Natural Science Foundation of China (51978569, 51908458), China Postdoctoral Science Foundation (2019M650264), and Xi'an Beilin District Science and Technology Plan Project of China (GX2019).Y. Su acknowledge the "Young Talent Support Plan" of Xi'an Jiaotong University and the supercomputing facilities supported by HPC Platform, Xi'an Jiaotong University. The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https:doi.org10.1016j.ijhydene.2021.01.024. i n t e r n a t i o n a l j o u r n a l o f h y d r o g e n e n e r g y 4 6 ( 2 0 2 1 ) 1 1 6 2 1 e1 1 6 3 5

lamp.source: The experiments were done under visible light irradiation using 500 W xenon lamp with cutoff filter (l 400 nm) by dispersing 0.02 g photocatalyst in a mixed solution (40 mL DMF, 5 mL triethanolamine-TEOA and 3 mL H 2 O).
lamp.context: 2-amino-terephthalic acid and zirconium (IV) chloride were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich, Reagent-grade N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF) and methanol were obtained from the Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd and used without treatment.Chloroauric Acid was also purchased from the Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.Graphene was got from the Nanjing XFNANO Materials Tech Co.,Ltd. As a reference, the AuUiO-66-NH 2 was obtained through the first procedure of fabricating the UiO-66-NH 2 , then the 60 mg UiO-66-NH 2 was adopted to deposite the Au nanoparticles, the experimental parameters were kept identical with previous. X-Ray Diffraction (XRD) patterns of the samples were collected on X'pert MPDPro (Panalytical Co.) diffractometer using Cu Ka radiation (40 kV, 40 mA) to analyze the crystal phase of the samples, 2 theta varied from 5 to 80 .Surface electronic states of the samples were performed by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS, Thermo Scientific EscaLab 250Xi, Al-Ka), the binding energy values were calibrated using C 1s 14 284.6 eV as a reference.Morphologies of the products were studied through the field emission scanning electron microscopy (SEM), instrument: HITACHI SU6600 (Japan).More detailed information on the morphologies and energy dispersive spectrometer (EDS) spectra were acquired from a JEOL JEM2100 (Japan) high resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM).The Brunauer Emmett Teller specific surface areas (BET) were studied on a Micromeritics ASAP 2020 device (USA) operating at liquid nitrogen temperature of A196 C. UVevisible (UVevis) diffused reflectance spectroscopies (DRS) were tested on a HITACHI UV4100 spectrometer (800 nm200 nm), reference: BaSO 4 .The apparent quantum yield was measured on Edinburgh FLS9 (China), Excitation range: 373 nme391 nm; Luminescence range: 392 nme801 nm. The electrochemical measurements were conducted on an electrochemical workstation (CHI 660D) in a three-electrode system.The photocatalyst was mixed with polyethylene glycol (PEG, MW 20000) and ethanol, then coated on the conductive surface of the FTO (active area of ca.4.0 cm 2 ) and served as the working electrode, before used, the coated FTO conductive glasses were dried at room temperature and calcined in a tube furnace (200 C, 2 h, N 2 atmosphere).The Platinum sheet was used as a counter electrode, a 500 W Xe lamp (l 400 nm) was employed as the light source, 0.50 molL Na 2 SO 4 aqueous solution was used as the electrolyte. The experiments were done under visible light irradiation using 500 W xenon lamp with cutoff filter (l 400 nm) by dispersing 0.02 g photocatalyst in a mixed solution (40 mL DMF, 5 mL triethanolamine-TEOA and 3 mL H 2 O).The homemade glass reactor was filled with mixture solution and bubbled with the mixing gas (Ar: CO 2 14 1:1) for 30 min.Then the reactor was exposed to the visible light illumination for 4 h at ambient temperature.The CO 2 reduction amount was calculated based on the following equation: CO 2 reduction amount 14 CeC 0 , where C 0 and C refer to the CO 2 amount determined before and after the reaction." The CO 2 was analyzed through gas chromatograph (Agilent Technologies: 6890N) with TCD detector (5A molecular sieve columns).Helium (99.999) was used as the carrier gas.The amount of H 2 was also determined on this gas chromatograph using N 2 as the carrier gas. The liquid product of formic acid was detected by ion chromatography.Ion chromatography (IC-1100) is equipped with CD detector, RFC-30 on-line electrolytic leaching fluid generator, Dionex ionpac AS18 4 A 250 mm chromatographic column.

The experiments were done under visible light irradiation using 500 W xenon lamp with cutoff filter (l 400 nm) by dispersing 0.02 g photocatalyst in a mixed solution (40 mL DMF, 5 mL triethanolamine-TEOA and 3 mL H 2 O).The homemade glass reactor was filled with mixture solution and bubbled with the mixing gas (Ar: CO 2 14 1:1) for 30 min.Then the reactor was exposed to the visible light illumination for 4 h at ambient temperature.The CO 2 reduction amount was calculated based on the following equation: CO 2 reduction amount 14 CeC 0 , where C 0 and C refer to the CO 2 amount determined before and after the reaction." The CO 2 was analyzed through gas chromatograph (Agilent Technologies: 6890N) with TCD detector (5A molecular sieve columns).Helium (99.999) was used as the carrier gas.The amount of H 2 was also determined on this gas chromatograph using N 2 as the carrier gas. The liquid product of formic acid was detected by ion chromatography.Ion chromatography (IC-1100) is equipped with CD detector, RFC-30 on-line electrolytic leaching fluid generator, Dionex ionpac AS18 4 A 250 mm chromatographic column.

reactor type.source: The experiments were done under visible light irradiation using 500 W xenon lamp with cutoff filter (l 400 nm) by dispersing 0.02 g photocatalyst in a mixed solution (40 mL DMF, 5 mL triethanolamine-TEOA and 3 mL H 2 O).
reactor type.context: 2-amino-terephthalic acid and zirconium (IV) chloride were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich, Reagent-grade N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF) and methanol were obtained from the Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd and used without treatment.Chloroauric Acid was also purchased from the Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.Graphene was got from the Nanjing XFNANO Materials Tech Co.,Ltd. The composite photocatalyst UiO-66-NH 2 GR was fabricated according reported solvothermal method 43.In a typical synthesis, before the experiment, 50 mL N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF) was prepared firstly in a jar bottle, then 2.0 graphene (2.0 mg graphene) was added in the bottle.The solution was stirred and ultrasonicated for 30 min respectively, then the 90 mg 2-amino-terephthalic acid and 120 mg zirconium (IV) chloride were dissolved in the DMF, along with stirring for 15 min and ultrasonication for another 15 min.The formed mixed solution is then transferred to a 100 mL Teflonlined stainless-steel autoclave.The autoclave was heated in a hot oven at 120 C for 24 h.After cooling down to room temperature, the sample was washed and centrifugated through methanol, DMF and ethanol for several times and put it in the vacuum oven for 12 h at 60 C. The as-obtained composite photocatalyst was named as UiO-66-NH 2 GR. Preparation of photocatalysts AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR and Au UiO-66-NH 2 First, the HAuCl 4 aqueous solution was prepared.The 1 g of HAuCl 4 solid was dissolved in 500 mL deionized water with stirring (concentration of the solution is 2 gL).The solution was then transferred to 500 mL brown volume bottle, wrapped in tinfoil paper on the outer wall and carefully placed in 4 C refrigerator (prevented oxidation discoloration). A quantity of the composite photocatalyst mentioned above was weighed and dispersed in a complex solution containing 40 mL water and 10 mL of methanol (reducing agent).Afterwards, different content of the as prepared HAuCl 4 solution (Theoretical load volume is 2e10, The specific quantity is 2 14 1.255 mL; 4 14 2.51 mL; 6 14 3.765 mL; 8 14 5.02 mL; 10 14 6.275 mL; the weight of UiO-66-NH 2 GR is 60 mg) was added in the complex solution.The mixture was degassed under nitrogen for 15 min in the ice water bath.After that, the air intakes and vents are closed, then it was put under visible light with stirring for different time.The illumination time range is 15 mine90 min respectively.The obtained sample was then centrifuged and washed with water and ethanol for 3 times respectively.The final product was dried under vacuum drying oven at 80 C for 24 h.The as-obtained composite photocatalyst was named as AuUiO-66-NH 2 GR. As a reference, the AuUiO-66-NH 2 was obtained through the first procedure of fabricating the UiO-66-NH 2 , then the 60 mg UiO-66-NH 2 was adopted to deposite the Au nanoparticles, the experimental parameters were kept identical with previous. X-Ray Diffraction (XRD) patterns of the samples were collected on X'pert MPDPro (Panalytical Co.) diffractometer using Cu Ka radiation (40 kV, 40 mA) to analyze the crystal phase of the samples, 2 theta varied from 5 to 80 .Surface electronic states of the samples were performed by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS, Thermo Scientific EscaLab 250Xi, Al-Ka), the binding energy values were calibrated using C 1s 14 284.6 eV as a reference.Morphologies of the products were studied through the field emission scanning electron microscopy (SEM), instrument: HITACHI SU6600 (Japan).More detailed information on the morphologies and energy dispersive spectrometer (EDS) spectra were acquired from a JEOL JEM2100 (Japan) high resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM).The Brunauer Emmett Teller specific surface areas (BET) were studied on a Micromeritics ASAP 2020 device (USA) operating at liquid nitrogen temperature of A196 C. UVevisible (UVevis) diffused reflectance spectroscopies (DRS) were tested on a HITACHI UV4100 spectrometer (800 nm200 nm), reference: BaSO 4 .The apparent quantum yield was measured on Edinburgh FLS9 (China), Excitation range: 373 nme391 nm; Luminescence range: 392 nme801 nm. The electrochemical measurements were conducted on an electrochemical workstation (CHI 660D) in a three-electrode system.The photocatalyst was mixed with polyethylene glycol (PEG, MW 20000) and ethanol, then coated on the conductive surface of the FTO (active area of ca.4.0 cm 2 ) and served as the working electrode, before used, the coated FTO conductive glasses were dried at room temperature and calcined in a tube furnace (200 C, 2 h, N 2 atmosphere).The Platinum sheet was used as a counter electrode, a 500 W Xe lamp (l 400 nm) was employed as the light source, 0.50 molL Na 2 SO 4 aqueous solution was used as the electrolyte. The experiments were done under visible light irradiation using 500 W xenon lamp with cutoff filter (l 400 nm) by dispersing 0.02 g photocatalyst in a mixed solution (40 mL DMF, 5 mL triethanolamine-TEOA and 3 mL H 2 O).The homemade glass reactor was filled with mixture solution and bubbled with the mixing gas (Ar: CO 2 14 1:1) for 30 min.Then the reactor was exposed to the visible light illumination for 4 h at ambient temperature.The CO 2 reduction amount was calculated based on the following equation: CO 2 reduction amount 14 CeC 0 , where C 0 and C refer to the CO 2 amount determined before and after the reaction." The CO 2 was analyzed through gas chromatograph (Agilent Technologies: 6890N) with TCD detector (5A molecular sieve columns).Helium (99.999) was used as the carrier gas.The amount of H 2 was also determined on this gas chromatograph using N 2 as the carrier gas. The liquid product of formic acid was detected by ion chromatography.Ion chromatography (IC-1100) is equipped with CD detector, RFC-30 on-line electrolytic leaching fluid generator, Dionex ionpac AS18 4 A 250 mm chromatographic column.
